name: Taj Mahal
description: Immense mausoleum of white marble built in Agra 1631-1648 by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his favourite wife.

name: Ellora Caves
description: 34 monasteries and temples dug into a basalt cliff between the 6th and 11th century AD. Represents Buddhist, Hindu and Jain religious art.

name: Ajanta Caves
description: Masterpieces of Buddhist religious art whose paintings and sculptures influenced Indian art that followed.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: Collection of rock art linked to the cult of Shiva on an island near Mumbai. Main cave has five chambers with colossal statues.

name: Rock Shelters of Bhimbetka
description: Natural rock shelters displaying paintings from the Mesolithic period through to the historical period in the Vindhyan Mountains.

name: Hampi
description: Ruins of Vijayanagara, former capital of one of the greatest Hindu kingdoms. Outstanding example of Dravidian architecture including Vittala Temple.

name: Qutb Minar and its Monuments
description: The Qutb Minar, at 72.5 m, is the tallest brick minaret in the world. The surrounding funerary buildings are among the finest examples of early Indo-Islamic architecture.

name: Great Wall of China
description: Greatest military edifice in history. Built over many centuries from the 7th century BC to protect China from invaders.

name: Forbidden City
description: Imperial Palace of the Ming and Qing Dynasties, the largest and best-preserved imperial palace complex in the world, built 1406-1420.

name: Angkor
description: Most important archaeological site in South-East Asia containing remains of the Khmer Empire capitals stretching over 400 km2.

name: Borobudur Temple Compounds
description: 9th-century Mahayana Buddhist temple in Central Java decorated with 2,672 relief panels and 504 Buddha statues.

name: Historic City of Ayutthaya
description: Founded in 1350 as capital of the Siamese Kingdom, Ayutthaya rose to be one of the world largest and most cosmopolitan cities.

name: Historic Centre of Rome
description: Centre of the Roman Republic, then the Roman Empire, then capital of the Christian world. Contains the Colosseum and Roman Forum.

name: Venice and its Lagoon
description: Built on 118 small islands separated by canals, Venice is an astonishing architectural masterpiece featuring works by Titian, Tintoretto, and Veronese.

name: Acropolis Athens
description: Universal symbols of classical civilisation forming the greatest architectural and artistic complex bequeathed by Greek Antiquity to the world.

name: Stonehenge Avebury and Associated Sites
description: Among the most famous groups of megaliths in the world. Circles of menhirs whose astronomical significance is still being explored.

name: Palace of Versailles
description: Principal residence of the French kings from Louis XIV to Louis XVI. Transformed from a hunting lodge into a monumental palace for the whole court.

name: Chartres Cathedral
description: Built in the 12th and 13th centuries, marks the high point of French Gothic art and strongly influenced subsequent Gothic architecture in Europe.

name: Prehistoric Sites and Decorated Caves of the Vezere Valley
description: 147 sites containing the most remarkable group of prehistoric paintings, engravings and sculptures including the famous Lascaux cave.

name: Alhambra Generalife and Albayzin Granada
description: Royal city of the Nasrid emirs ruling the last Moorish kingdom in Iberia. Architecture and gardens reflect exceptional Hispano-Islamic culture.

name: Works of Antoni Gaudi
description: Extraordinary contribution to late 19th and early 20th century architecture. The Sagrada Familia is an expressionist synthesis of Gothic and Art Nouveau.

name: Cologne Cathedral
description: Gothic masterpiece begun in 1248 and completed in 1880, dedicated to St Peter and the Virgin Mary, one of the finest examples of Gothic architecture.

name: Kremlin and Red Square Moscow
description: Built in the 15th century on a hill above the Moscow River, serving as the symbolic heart of Russia for centuries.

name: Historic Centre of Prague
description: Comprising the Old Town, Mala Strana and New Town built between the 11th and 18th centuries, an outstanding example of interplaying architectural styles.

name: Yellowstone National Park
description: The world first national park, containing half of all the world geysers. Created by the largest supervolcanic system in North America.

name: Grand Canyon National Park
description: Natural landscape of exceptional beauty in the Colorado River valley, Arizona. Over 1.6 km deep, revealing 2 billion years of geological history.

name: Historic Sanctuary of Machu Picchu
description: Standing 2,430 m above sea-level in a tropical mountain forest, probably the most amazing urban creation of the Inca Empire.

name: Chichen-Itza
description: Most important city of the Maya civilization on the Yucatan Peninsula, the political and economic centre flourishing between the 9th and 12th centuries.

name: Pre-Hispanic City of Teotihuacan
description: Holy city built between the 1st and 7th centuries AD, characterized by the Temple of the Sun and Moon. One of the largest cities in the ancient world.

name: Rapa Nui National Park
description: Easter Island is an extraordinary testimony to a unique cultural tradition, containing the famous moai monolithic human figures erected by the Rapa Nui people.

name: Old Havana and its Fortification System
description: Founded in the 16th century, Havana was one of the most important bastions in the Spanish colonial empire. Its fortifications are the most complete military architecture in the Americas.

name: Galapagos Islands
description: Situated in the Pacific Ocean, the Galapagos Islands inspired Darwin theory of evolution and continue to be a living laboratory of evolution.

name: Memphis and its Necropolis
description: Capital of ancient Egypt at the height of its power, including the pyramid fields from Giza to Dahshur, outstanding proof of ancient Egyptian civilization.

name: Nubian Monuments from Abu Simbel to Philae
description: Contains the Temples of Ramses II at Abu Simbel and the Sanctuary of Isis at Philae. Unique artistic achievements saved from flooding by UNESCO.

name: Petra
description: Capital of the Nabataean Empire between the 4th century BC and the 2nd century AD. Half-built, half-carved in the rock, surrounded by mountains.

name: Persepolis
description: Founded by Darius I in 518 BC, Persepolis was the capital of the Achaemenid Empire and a unique testimony to Achaemenid civilization.

name: Old City of Jerusalem and its Walls
description: Jerusalem holds a unique position in the history of Judaism, Christianity and Islam, containing some of the most sacred sites of these three religions.

name: Great Barrier Reef
description: The world most extensive stretch of coral reef on the north-east coast of Australia, home to 1,500 species of fish and 4,000 types of mollusc.

name: Serengeti National Park
description: The vast plains of the Serengeti comprise 1.5 million ha of savannah. The annual migration of wildebeest is one of the most spectacular events in nature.

name: Kilimanjaro National Park
description: Mount Kilimanjaro is the highest peak in Africa at 5,895 m, encompassing vegetation from forest to alpine desert and hosting rare endemic species.

name: Timbuktu
description: A major intellectual and spiritual capital and centre for Islam throughout Africa in the 15th and 16th centuries, containing three great mosques.
